name: Sam
manager_notes: Sam is in week three and has started picking up real tickets, but he still hesitates before shipping and asks for reassurance late. I need to understand whether this is a normal confidence dip or a tooling and expectation gap we can fix quickly. I also want clarity on where onboarding handoffs are still fuzzy so he is not quietly compensating. This should stay practical and supportive: map what is unclear, unblock him, and line up the right support touchpoints for the next two weeks.
answers: Can do the tasks, but second-guesses whether the approach matches team expectations.
Local setup better now, but loses time when design and API details don't match.
Not always sure who should approve what before pushing.
Usually waits too long before asking — wants to solve things himself first.
When feedback is specific and fast, moves much quicker.
Still fuzzy on where quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows.
Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent getting stuck silently.
With good defined for the next two tickets, would feel much steadier.
Wants to start by pairing on the next ticket to see the approval bar in practice.
